title: delegation — trait composition
description: Two owned traits. HasDelegations composes on the tenant `User` model + exposes the outbound + inbound delegation relations plus fluent helpers. HasImpersonationHistory is dual-use — composes on BOTH tenant `User` (the impersonated party's retrospective view) AND PlatformUser (the impersonator's history of sessions they operated).

owned.description: Mixed into the tenant `User` model. Adds `outboundDelegations()` (as delegator) + `inboundDelegations()` (as delegate) + `activeOutboundDelegations()` (scoped to now-within-window + not-revoked) + `activeInboundDelegations()` (same, from delegate perspective). Also adds fluent helpers `delegate(User $to, ?Role $role, DateTimeInterface $ends_at, string $reason, DelegationType $type = OOF)` for creating a delegation + `revokeDelegation(string $id, string $reason)` for early revocation. The active-* scopes are the primary reads for the /me/delegations UI + the DelegationResolver service.

Dual-use trait mixed into BOTH tenant `User` AND PlatformUser. On the User side, exposes `impersonationSessionsAsTarget()` — historical sessions where they were impersonated (the affected party's retrospective view). On the PlatformUser side, exposes `impersonationSessionsAsOperator()` — sessions they initiated (their operator history, used by the audit + the platform-admin UI). Both relations point at `impersonation_sessions` via different FK columns (`impersonated_user_id` vs `impersonator_platform_user_id`) so no relation collision. Snapshot fields (impersonator_display_name + impersonator_department) preserve the operator relation view even after the PlatformUser row hard-deletes.

framework.reason: RoleDelegation ONLY. NOT ImpersonationSession — impersonation sessions are append-only compliance evidence + never soft-deleted.

activity.reason: RoleDelegation ONLY. Lightweight activity log for the delegator + delegate audit trail. Not on ImpersonationSession — impersonation uses HasAudit's heavier trail for compliance evidence.

audit.reason: BOTH RoleDelegation AND ImpersonationSession — but ImpersonationSession's audit rows are flagged with `is_compliance_evidence=true` so the retention job leaves them untouched at 7-year hot (see retention.json).

role_delegation.notes: BelongsToTenant MUST come first per tenancy-columns.md §14 note — subsequent traits observe the tenant context in their booted() hooks. Soft-deleted so a revoked delegation stays queryable through the 730-day retention window before PurgeRevokedDelegationsJob hard-deletes.

impersonation_session.notes: NO SoftDeletes — impersonation session rows are append-only compliance evidence + preserved indefinitely. NO Searchable (the surface is opinionated + we already publish the /audit endpoint for cross-cutting query). NO HasActivityLog (that's the tenant-side activity substrate; impersonation belongs to the heavier HasAudit compliance substrate). BelongsToTenant applies via target_tenant_id — the global scope filters by the caller's current tenant on tenant-plane reads (there are none — this table is platform-plane-read-only), but on platform_admin routes the scope is bypassed by the RolesPermissions middleware since a platform admin is cross-tenant by construction.
